lede: A look at March 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Yuri Gagarin and Julia Mancuso, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Barbie
events.desc: The Barbie doll makes its debut at the American International Toy Fair in New York.

events.title: 1957 Andreanof Islands earthquake
events.desc: The 8.6 Mw Andreanof Islands earthquake shakes the Aleutian Islands, causing over $5 million in damage from ground movement and a destructive tsunami.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Dutch East Indies unconditionally surrenders to the Japanese forces in Kalijati, Subang, West Java, and the Japanese complete their Dutch East Indies campaign.

events.title: Inter Milan
events.desc: Inter Milan is founded on Football Club Internazionale, following a schism from A.C. Milan.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: USS Monitor and CSS Virginia (rebuilt from the engines and lower hull of the USS Merrimack) fight to a draw in the Battle of Hampton Roads, the first battle between two ironclad warships.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: The first large-scale amphibious assault in U.S. history is launched in the Siege of Veracruz.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoléon Bonaparte marries his first wife, Joséphine de Beauharnais.

events.title: Adam Smith
events.desc: Scottish philosopher Adam Smith publishes The Wealth of Nations, ushering in the classical period of political economy.

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgarian Tsar Ivan Asen II defeats Theodore of Epirus in the Battle of Klokotnitsa.

events.title: Lithuania
events.desc: First known mention of Lithuania, in the annals of the monastery of Quedlinburg.

births.name: Yuri Gagarin
births.line: Russian colonel, pilot, and cosmonaut, first human in space

births.name: John Cale
births.line: Welsh musician, composer, singer, songwriter and record producer

births.name: Amerigo Vespucci
births.line: Italian cartographer and explorer, namesake of the Americas

births.name: Emma Bonino
births.line: Italian politician, Italian Minister of Foreign Affairs

deaths.text: Kurt Georg Kiesinger, German lawyer, politician and Chancellor of Germany
Earl Wilbur Sutherland Jr., Jr., American pharmacologist and biochemist, Nobel Prize laureate
Hans Christian Ørsted, Danish physicist and chemist, discovered electromagnetism and the element aluminium
Abu Ma'shar al-Balkhi, Muslim scholar and astrologer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Forty Martyrs of Sebaste
Christian feast day: Pope Cyril VI of Alexandria (Coptic Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Gregory of Nyssa (Episcopal Church (United States))
